paper_title: Palynological and LOI Analysis of EYK and BBH Cores

methods.description: Analysis of pollen, spores, and non-pollen palynomorphs (NPPs) to reconstruct past vegetation, climatic, palaeoenvironmental changes, and anthropogenic impacts on ecosystems.

steps.description: 39 subsamples of 1 cm³ volume were taken along the EYK core and 23 subsamples along the BBH core.
steps.searchable_summary: collect subsamples from EYK and BBH cores

steps.description: One tablet with exotic Lycopodium (containing 9666 ± 212 spores) was added for pollen concentration and influx calculations.
The subsamples were sieved through 120 μm metal mesh to get rid of large particles.
HCl (~ 10%) was added to remove the calcareous content and cold HF (~ 40%) treatment was applied to remove the siliceous content.
steps.searchable_summary: apply HCl and HF treatments to remove calcareous and siliceous content

steps.description: Pollen, spores, and NPPs were concentrated by sieving through a 10 μm nylon mesh.
steps.searchable_summary: sieve processed subsamples through 10 μm mesh

steps.description: Pollen identification was performed using published pollen morphological literature and reference collections.
steps.searchable_summary: identify pollen using literature and reference collections

steps.description: Pollen taxa were grouped into trees/shrubs, anthropogenic indicators, herbs, and wetland taxa.
Data are illustrated by TILIA and TILIAGRAPH; CONISS cluster analysis was used for the identification of pollen diagram zonation.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize data using TILIA and TILIAGRAPH, perform CONISS cluster analysis

methods.description: Standard procedure to estimate the carbonate and organic content in the EYK core using LOI analysis.

steps.description: 40 subsamples with a volume of 1 cm³ sediment have been taken along the core.
The wet weighted samples were dried up to 105 °C in a muffle furnace for 24 h.
steps.searchable_summary: dry samples in muffle furnace at 105 °C for 24 hours

steps.description: After cooling down to room temperature, the weight of the dried material was measured.
The dried samples were heated in the furnace up to 550 °C and then burned for 4 h and after cooling measured again.
steps.searchable_summary: heat samples to 550 °C, burn for 4 hours, measure weight

steps.description: The weight was measured again after furnace heating and burning up to 950 °C.

The organic (550 °C) and carbonate (950 °C) content with LOI measurement was calculated using the following equation respectively: LOI 550 °C = ((DW 105 °C -DW 550 °C )/DW 105 °C ) * 100; LOI 950 °C = ((DW 550 °C -DW 950 °C )/DW 105 °C ) * 100.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate LOI for organic and carbonate content
